# ReFormer Mcp - LLM Integration Guide
# AUTO-GENERATED. Edit docs/llms/*.md or JSDoc in src/ and run npm run generate:llms.

> MCP server for ReFormer form library - provides AI assistants with comprehensive documentation and tools for form development
> Package: @reformer/mcp  ‚Ä¢  Version: 6.0.0

## Table of Contents
- 01-guide.md ‚Äî ReFormer MCP ‚Äî start-here guide
- 02-tools.md ‚Äî Tools
- 03-prompts.md ‚Äî Prompts
- 04-resources.md ‚Äî Resources
- 05-m1-workflow.md ‚Äî M1 form-building workflow
- 06-form-directory-layout.md ‚Äî Form directory layout (core / renderer-react / renderer-json)

## 1. How to use it (recommended order)

1. **Get oriented** ‚Äî run the `start-here` prompt (or read this guide). It returns the M1
   workflow and the map below.
2. **Plan** ‚Äî for a form from a spec, use the `plan-form` prompt (it reads and parses the spec
   file). For a free-text description, use `create-form`. To detect the target stack, `discover-context`.
3. **Read the workflow** ‚Äî the M1 build workflow (model ‚Üí schema ‚Üí validation ‚Üí behaviors ‚Üí arrays ‚Üí
   wizard ‚Üí render). It is part of this guide; the whole self-doc is one resource: `reformer://guide`.
4. **Look things up as you code**:
   - `find_recipe <topic>` ‚Äî a worked example for a scenario (e.g. `wizard`, `form-array`, `cycle`, `json-schema`).
   - `get_symbol_docs <name>` ‚Äî exact signature + `@example` of a function/type (e.g. `createForm`, `validateFormModel`).
   - `list_symbols` ‚Äî the API surface by kind/package when you don't know the name.
5. **Add features** with the focused prompts: `add-validation`, `add-behavior`, `add-form-array`, `add-wizard`.
6. **Migrate render target**: `to-renderer` (core ‚Üí renderer-react), `to-renderer-json` (react ‚Üí JSON).
7. **Check your work**:
   - `check_behaviors` ‚Äî declare compute/copy dependencies, get cycle detection.
   - `validate_json_schema` ‚Äî validate a renderer-json JSON schema before rendering.
   - `review` ‚Äî cross-package code-review checklist.

## 2. What's where

The full self-doc of this server is one resource ‚Äî `reformer://guide` (aka `reformer://docs/mcp`) ‚Äî
covering: **Tools** (callable lookup + validation), **Prompts** (one per workflow step),
**Resources** (`reformer://docs/<pkg>[/<section>]` for the 5 library packages), and the **M1 workflow**.
Individual sections are addressable per-heading (slug from the H2 title); enumerate exact URIs with ListResources.

## 3. The 5 library packages (read their docs via resources)

- `@reformer/core` ‚Äî model, schema, validation, behaviors (`reformer://docs/core`).
- `@reformer/cdk` ‚Äî headless FormArray / FormWizard / FormField compounds (`reformer://docs/cdk`).
- `@reformer/ui-kit` ‚Äî styled field components: Input, Select, Checkbox‚Ä¶ (`reformer://docs/ui-kit`).
- `@reformer/renderer-react` ‚Äî declarative RenderSchema (`reformer://docs/renderer-react`).
- `@reformer/renderer-json` ‚Äî JSON schema + registry (`reformer://docs/renderer-json`).

## 4. Golden rules

- **M1**: one `createModel` is the source of truth; schema leaves reference its signals (`value: model.$.x`).
- Validation runs through `validateFormModel(model, schema)`, not `form.validate()`.
- The removed path-based API (`validate(path.x)`, `RenderSchemaFn = (path) =>`, `ValidationSchemaFn`) is gone ‚Äî don't use it.
- Prefer `createForm({ model, schema })` over legacy overloads.

## 5. get_symbol_docs

Full JSDoc for one public symbol of any `@reformer/*` package: description, signature,
params, `@returns`, every `@example`, source path.
- `symbol` (string, required) ‚Äî e.g. `"createForm"`, `"validateFormModel"`, `"FormArray"`.
- `package` (string, optional) ‚Äî e.g. `"@reformer/core"`; omit to search all.

Use before writing code against an unfamiliar symbol.

## 6. find_recipe

A worked example / how-to for a scenario. Cascade: docs/llms filename ‚Üí `##` section ‚Üí
symbol `@example` ‚Üí fallback list.
- `topic` (string, required) ‚Äî keyword. Aliases resolve intuitive terms: `wizard`‚Üímulti-step,
  `form-array`‚Üíarrays, `cycle`‚Üícycle-detection, `cross-field`‚Üísync-fields/copy-from, `json-schema`, etc.
- `package` (string, optional).

Use to copy a correct pattern instead of guessing.

## 7. list_symbols

The API surface by kind and package ‚Äî discovery when you don't know a name.
- `kind` (optional) ‚Äî `function` | `class` | `interface` | `type` | `const` | `enum`.
- `package` (optional) ‚Äî one package or `*`.

E.g. all functions of `@reformer/core` enumerate every validator and behavior. Then
`get_symbol_docs` the one you want.

## 8. validate_json_schema

Validate a `@reformer/renderer-json` form-DSL JSON schema **before rendering**. Checks node
structure, operator syntax (`$model`/`$component`/`$dataSource`), and unknown component/
data-source names.
- `schema` (object | JSON string, required) ‚Äî the schema you generated.
- `componentNames` (string[], optional) ‚Äî names in your registry, enables unknown-`$component(...)` detection.
- `dataSourceNames` (string[], optional) ‚Äî enables unknown-`$dataSource(...)` detection.

Returns `{ valid, errors[] }`. Always run it on a renderer-json schema before handing it off.

## 9. check_behaviors

Static cycle detection for reactive behaviors. Declare, per computed/copied field, what it reads;
the tool finds cyclic dependencies (the loops the runtime throws "Cycle detected" for).
- `dependencies` (required) ‚Äî array of `{ target: string, reads: string[] }`.

E.g. `[{ target: "total", reads: ["price","quantity"] }]`. Run after planning compute/copyFrom.

## 10. report_issue

Record a ReFormer problem + its fix to a local knowledge store (`~/.reformer/issues.jsonl`).
- `error` (string, required), `solution` (string, required), optional `tags`, `context`.

Use when you discover and fix a non-obvious ReFormer error, to help future runs.

---

_A `debug` tool exists only when the server runs with `REFORMER_DEBUG=true`; ignore it in normal use._

## 11. start-here

No arguments. The entry point: returns the M1 workflow, the map of prompts/tools/resources,
and the reading order. Call it first when asked to build or modify a form.

## 12. discover-context

- `description` (required), `projectPath` (optional).

Detects the target stack (ui-kit / Tailwind / which renderer) from the consumer project and
recommends a render target. Optional first step when the target isn't given.

## 13. plan-form

- `specPath` (required), `target` (optional: core | renderer-react | renderer-json), `projectPath` (optional).

Reads and parses a markdown spec file, then returns a roadmap: steps, fields, conditionals,
behaviors, API endpoints, a risk matrix, and verification scenarios. Use when the form comes
from a written spec.

## 14. create-form

- `description` (required), `target` (optional, default core), `projectPath` (optional).

Turns a free-text form description into build instructions for the chosen target (quick-start,
FormSchema reference, imports, stack-aware skeleton). Use for the initial form when there is no spec file.

## 15. add-validation

- `code` (existing FormSchema, required), `requirements` (required).

Adds validators (built-in, custom cross-field `ModelValidator`, async) to an existing form.

## 16. add-behavior

- `code` (required), `requirements` (required).

Wires reactive behaviors: `compute`, `copyFrom`, `enableWhen`, `onChange`, etc. Pair with the
`check_behaviors` tool to rule out cycles.

## 17. add-form-array

- `code` (required), `requirements` (required).

Turns a field into a dynamic array: schema `{ array, item, initialValue }` + the CDK `FormArray` compound.

## 18. add-wizard

- `code` (required), `steps` (required).

Turns a single-page form into a multi-step `FormWizard` with a `FormWizardConfig` `{ validateStep, validateAll }`.

## 19. to-renderer / to-renderer-json

- `code` (required).

Migrate render target: `to-renderer` (core manual JSX ‚Üí renderer-react RenderSchema),
`to-renderer-json` (renderer-react RenderSchema ‚Üí renderer-json JSON + registry). After
`to-renderer-json`, validate the result with `validate_json_schema`.

## 20. review

- `code` (required).

Cross-package review checklist (state setup, integration, anti-patterns) for existing form code.

---

_A `debug` prompt exists only with `REFORMER_DEBUG=true`._

## 21. URI scheme

- `reformer://guide` ‚Äî this server's full self-doc (workflow + tools + prompts + resources). The entry point in resource form (mirrors the `start-here` prompt).
- `reformer://docs/<pkg>` ‚Äî the full `llms.txt` of one library package (every section concatenated).
- `reformer://docs/<pkg>/<section-slug>` ‚Äî a single `##` section of a package, by slug.

`<pkg>` is a short name: `core`, `cdk`, `ui-kit`, `renderer-react`, `renderer-json`, `mcp`.

## 22. Self-documentation (this server)

- `reformer://guide` ‚Äî the whole self-doc in one document (guide + tools + prompts + resources + workflow).
  Equivalent to `reformer://docs/mcp` (the full mcp `llms.txt`).
- Individual sections are addressable, but their slug is derived from the section's H2 heading, not the
  filename. Enumerate exact URIs with ListResources. Examples of real slugs:
  `reformer://docs/mcp/validate-json-schema`, `reformer://docs/mcp/schema-field-leaves`,
  `reformer://docs/mcp/checklist-before-you-finish`.

## 23. Library docs (the 5 packages)

Each package's sections come from its `docs/llms/*.md`. Discover exact slugs with ListResources.
Examples:

- `reformer://docs/core` ‚Äî model, schema, validators, behaviors, arrays, multi-step, common mistakes (~50 sections).
- `reformer://docs/cdk/form-array`, `reformer://docs/cdk/form-navigation` ‚Äî FormArray / FormWizard compounds.
- `reformer://docs/ui-kit/form-field-integration`, `reformer://docs/ui-kit/choice-fields` ‚Äî components.
- `reformer://docs/renderer-react/render-schema`, `.../render-behavior` ‚Äî RenderSchema.
- `reformer://docs/renderer-json/json-schema`, `.../registry` ‚Äî JSON DSL + registry.

## 24. How this relates to tools

`find_recipe` reads the same `docs/llms/*.md` directly (with alias resolution); resources give
you the raw sections when you want to browse or read a whole package. When you know the scenario,
`find_recipe` is faster; when you want the full section text, read the resource.

## 25. 0. Choose the render target

- **core + ui-kit** ‚Äî plain React: one `<FormField control={form.x} />` per field. Simplest.
- **renderer-react** ‚Äî declarative `RenderSchema` tree (layout + conditional display as data).
- **renderer-json** ‚Äî machine-readable JSON schema + a component `registry` (string operators).

All three share steps 1‚Äì6; they differ only at step 7.

## 26. 1. Model ‚Äî `createModel<T>(initial)`

The model owns all values. Decisions: the data shape as a `type` (NOT `interface` ‚Äî see
`find_recipe type-safety-recipes`), and full initial values.

- Numbers optional ‚Üí `null`; strings ‚Üí `''`; arrays ‚Üí `[]`.
- Array items must initialise **every** field, or the item's sub-model has no signals for the missing ones.
- Stabilise the instance in `useMemo` so it isn't rebuilt each render.

```ts
import { createModel } from '@reformer/core';
type RegForm = { email: string; password: string; age: number | null };
const model = createModel<RegForm>({ email: '', password: '', age: null });
// model.email (value) ¬∑ model.$.email (signal) ¬∑ model.get() ¬∑ model.set(partial)
```

## 27. 2. Schema ‚Äî field leaves

The schema is a tree; each **field leaf** carries value-signal + component + props + validators.
Layout (Step/Section/Grid) stays in React, not in the schema.

```ts
import { Input } from '@reformer/ui-kit';
import { required, email, minLength } from '@reformer/core/validators';
const schema = {
  children: [
    { value: model.$.email, component: Input, componentProps: { label: 'Email' }, validators: [required(), email()] },
    { value: model.$.password, component: Input, componentProps: { label: 'Password' }, validators: [required(), minLength(8)] },
  ],
};
```

Traps: `value: model.$.field` (a signal) ‚Äî never `value: 'field'`. Don't forget `componentProps.label`.

## 28. 3. Create the form ‚Äî `createForm({ model, schema })`

```ts
import { createForm } from '@reformer/core';
const form = createForm<RegForm>({ model, schema }); // FormProxy: form.email is a node bound to model.$.email
```

Overloads `createForm({ form: {...} })` and `createForm(flatSchema)` are legacy ‚Äî use `{ model, schema }`.

## 29. 4. Validation ‚Äî `validateFormModel(model, schema)`

- Value-only validators: `required()`, `email()`, `min(50000)` ‚Äî factories from `@reformer/core/validators`.
- Cross-field / async: a custom `ModelValidator` `(value, scope, root) => ValidationError | null` (or `Promise<...>`), placed in a field's `validators`.
- Run all validators with `validateFormModel(model, schema)` ‚Üí `{ valid, errors }`. It ‚Äî not `form.validate()` ‚Äî executes schema validators.

```ts
import { validateFormModel, type ModelValidator } from '@reformer/core';
const passwordsMatch: ModelValidator<string, unknown, RegForm> =
  (value, _scope, root) => (value !== root.password ? { code: 'mismatch', message: 'Passwords differ' } : null);
```

## 30. 5. Behaviors ‚Äî reactive dynamics (optional)

Computed / copied / conditionally-enabled fields. Two styles: DSL `defineFormBehavior` + operators,
or primitives in a `useEffect`. Register the DSL via `createForm({ model, schema, behavior })`.

```ts
import { defineFormBehavior } from '@reformer/core/behaviors';
const behavior = defineFormBehavior<OrderForm>(({ model, form }) => {
  compute(model.$.total, () => model.price * model.quantity);          // reads OTHER fields, writes its own
  copyFrom(model.$.registration, model.$.residence, { when: () => model.sameAddress });
  enableWhen(form.residence, () => model.sameAddress === false);        // state op ‚Äî affects the node, not the value
});
```

Trap: **cycles** (`compute(a, () => a)` or `a‚Üíb‚Üía`) loop forever. Plan dependencies and run them
through the `check_behaviors` tool; see `find_recipe cycle`.

## 31. 6. Arrays & Wizard (when needed)

- **Array** node in the schema: `{ array: model.items, item: (im) => itemSchema, initialValue: {...full...} }`.
  In React use the `@reformer/cdk` `FormArray` compound; **key rows by `id`, not index**. `find_recipe form-array`.
- **Wizard**: `@reformer/cdk` `FormWizardConfig = { validateStep?, validateAll? }` ‚Äî two callbacks returning
  `boolean | Promise<boolean>` (NOT schemas). `find_recipe wizard`.

## 32. 7. Render

- **ui-kit**: `<FormField control={form.email} />` per field.
- **renderer-react**: `createRenderSchema<T>(() => renderNodeTree)`, conditional display via `hideWhen(node, () => cond)`, mount with `<FormRenderer render={schema} settings={{ fieldWrapper: FormField }} />`. `find_recipe render-schema`.
- **renderer-json**: JSON with string operators `$model(path)` / `$component(Name)` / `$dataSource(NAME)`,
  a `defineRegistry` mapping names ‚Üí components, `convertJsonToM1Tree(json, registry, model)`, `<JsonRendererProvider settings={{ registry, model }}>` + `<JsonFormRenderer schema={json} />`.
  **Validate the JSON with the `validate_json_schema` tool before rendering.** `find_recipe json-schema`.

### Validation is on the model, not the render tree (renderer-react / renderer-json)

The render tree (RenderSchema or JSON) describes **layout**. It does NOT carry validators ‚Äî
`JsonFieldNode` has no `validators`, and a RenderSchema leaf is display config. Validation lives
on the **model**: build a field schema `{ children: [{ value: model.$.x, validators: [...] }] }`,
pass it to `createForm({ model, schema })`, and run `validateFormModel(model, schema)` at submit.
So for renderer targets you keep two things bound to the same model: the **field schema** (values +
validators, for `createForm`/`validateFormModel`) and the **render tree** (layout, for the renderer).
For a wizard, the field schema is flat (all fields), independent of how steps nest them in the render tree.

## 33. Checklist before you finish

1. Model initialises every field (incl. array-item fields). 2. Leaves are `{ value: model.$.x, component, ... }`.
3. Validators are factories/`ModelValidator`; run via `validateFormModel`. 4. Behaviors are acyclic (`check_behaviors`).
5. Arrays keyed by `id`. 6. renderer-json schema passed `validate_json_schema` ‚Üí `valid`.

## 34. 1. Overview ‚Äî what is shared vs target-specific

| Layer                     | core (+ ui-kit)              | renderer-react            | renderer-json                     |
| ------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| Types / constants / api   | shared                       | reuse                     | reuse                             |
| `model.ts`                | shared                       | reuse                     | reuse                             |
| `validation.ts`           | shared                       | reuse                     | reuse                             |
| `behavior.ts` (model)     | shared                       | reuse                     | reuse                             |
| `utils/compute/`          | shared                       | reuse                     | reuse                             |
| Field/layout tree         | `schema.ts` + step components | `render-schema.ts`        | `json-schema.json` + `registry.ts` |
| Render behavior           | ‚Äî (React components)         | `render-behavior.ts`      | thin `render-behavior.ts` ‚Üí shared |
| Entry component           | `[FormName]Form.tsx`         | `[FormName]FormRenderer.tsx` | `[FormName]FormRendererJson.tsx` |

Rule of thumb: **put anything that isn't layout into the shared core.** Renderer variants
should import the core (types, model, validation, behavior, constants, api, compute, ui
components), not re-declare it.

## 35. 2. Shared core (all targets)

```
[form-name]/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ types/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ [form-name].ts        # main form interface + field enums (LoanType, EmploymentStatus‚Ä¶)
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ option.ts             # shared { value, label } option type
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ constants/
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ [form-name].ts        # option dictionaries (LOAN_TYPES, GENDERS, ‚Ä¶) used by schema + behavior
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ api/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ index.ts              # re-exports
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ fetch-*.ts            # data sources (dictionaries, regions, cities, ‚Ä¶) + a mock for dev
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ submit-*.ts           # submit endpoint
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ utils/compute/
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ compute-*.ts          # pure functions for derived fields (age, monthlyPayment, totalIncome‚Ä¶)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ model.ts                  # createModel factory + initial values + blank-element factories for arrays
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ validation.ts             # validators + validateFormModel config ‚Üí { validateStep, validateAll }
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ behavior.ts               # defineFormBehavior: compute / copyFrom / enableWhen / onChange
```

- **`model.ts`** is the source of truth (`FormModel<[FormName]>`); every target binds to its signals.
- **`validation.ts`** and **`behavior.ts`** are target-agnostic ‚Äî the same reactive rules power all three.
- Split `utils/compute/*` one function per derived field so `behavior.ts` reads cleanly.

## 36. 3. Target: core (+ ui-kit)

Adds the field tree as a `FormSchema` plus React components for layout.

```
[form-name]/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ‚Ä¶shared core (section 2)‚Ä¶
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ schema.ts                 # FormSchema tree: { value: model.$.x, component, componentProps }
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ create-form.ts            # assembly: createForm({ model, schema, behavior }) ‚Üí { model, form }
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ operators.ts              # optional: custom behavior operators (loadOptionsOn, clearWhenOff‚Ä¶)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ components/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ steps/                # one component per wizard step (BasicInfoForm, ‚Ä¶)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ nested-forms/         # reusable sub-forms (Address, PersonalData, ‚Ä¶)
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ ui/                   # helper blocks (summary, warnings, sections)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ hooks/                    # React hooks (load data, copy address, ‚Ä¶)
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ [FormName]Form.tsx        # entry: builds model+form, renders FormWizard + step components
```

## 37. 4. Target: renderer-react

Layout lives in a `RenderSchema` tree (data, not JSX), so per-step components are not needed.
`components/ui/` blocks are still reused.

```
[form-name]/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ‚Ä¶shared core (section 2)‚Ä¶
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ render-schema.ts          # RenderNode tree: containers (Step/Section/Box) + field leaves
‚îÇ                             # created via createRenderSchema(() => buildSchema(model))
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ render-behavior.ts        # hideWhen / renderEffect / navigation / submit / data-loading
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ [FormName]FormRenderer.tsx # entry: builds model+form, renders <FormRenderer />
```

- Leaves carry the **model signal** (`{ value: model.$.x, component, componentProps }`), never the resolved `form.X`.
- Give a `selector` to any node you manipulate programmatically (`hideWhen`, `patchProps`).

## 38. 5. Target: renderer-json

Layout is a static JSON document (string operators) plus a component registry. Runtime
entities (a `FormProxy`, a validation config) are injected via a thin render-behavior.

```
[form-name]/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ‚Ä¶shared core (section 2)‚Ä¶
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ json-schema.json          # layout as JSON: "$model(x)" / "$component(Name)" / "$dataSource(NAME)"
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ form-schema.schema.json    # JSON Schema validating json-schema.json structure
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ registry.ts               # component + dataSource registry (names ‚Üí components / option arrays / fns)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ render-behavior.ts        # thin: onInit injects form + validation into the wizard, then delegates
‚îÇ                             # to the shared renderer-react render-behavior
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ [FormName]FormRendererJson.tsx # entry: convertJsonToM1Tree(json, registry, model) + <JsonFormRenderer />
```

- The JSON is loadable from a string (server/CMS/file); `registry.ts` maps `$component(...)` / `$dataSource(...)` to real components and values.
- The render-behavior is intentionally thin ‚Äî it reuses the renderer-react behavior so visibility/navigation/submit logic is defined once.

## 39. 6. Reuse map

| File / folder            | Role                                              | core | renderer-react | renderer-json |
| ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------- | :--: | :------------: | :-----------: |
| `types/`, `constants/`   | data interface + option dictionaries              |  ‚úÖ  |     reuse      |     reuse     |
| `api/`, `utils/compute/` | data sources + derived-field functions            |  ‚úÖ  |     reuse      |     reuse     |
| `model.ts`               | reactive model (source of truth)                  |  ‚úÖ  |     reuse      |     reuse     |
| `validation.ts`          | validators + validateStep/validateAll config      |  ‚úÖ  |     reuse      |     reuse     |
| `behavior.ts`            | model behavior (compute/copyFrom/enableWhen)      |  ‚úÖ  |     reuse      |     reuse     |
| `schema.ts`              | FormSchema field tree                             |  ‚úÖ  |       ‚Äî        |       ‚Äî       |
| `components/steps,nested-forms` | per-step / sub-form React components        |  ‚úÖ  |       ‚Äî        |       ‚Äî       |
| `components/ui/`         | helper UI blocks                                  |  ‚úÖ  |     reuse      |     reuse     |
| `render-schema.ts`       | RenderNode layout tree                            |  ‚Äî   |       ‚úÖ       |       ‚Äî       |
| `render-behavior.ts`     | visibility/navigation/submit                      |  ‚Äî   |       ‚úÖ       | thin ‚Üí shared |
| `json-schema.json` + `registry.ts` | JSON layout + component/dataSource registry |  ‚Äî   |       ‚Äî        |      ‚úÖ       |

## 40. 7. Scaling

| Complexity | Structure                                                                                   |
| ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Simple     | Single file: `[FormName]Form.tsx` (model + schema + component)                               |
| Medium     | Split shared core: `types.ts`, `model.ts`, `schema.ts`, `validation.ts`, entry component    |
| Complex    | Full layout above: `behavior.ts`, `constants/`, `api/`, `utils/compute/`, plus the target's presentation layer (`schema.ts` + `components/` **or** `render-schema.ts` **or** `json-schema.json` + `registry.ts`) |

For a single target keep everything in one `[form-name]/` module. To ship the same form on
several targets, keep the shared core once and add the per-target presentation files (or
per-target subfolders) that import it ‚Äî never duplicate model/validation/behavior.
